sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Nottingham Forest, currently sitting at 6th in the league with 60 points, host Brentford, who are 11th with 49 points, on May 1, 2025, at 18:30 at the City Ground in Nottingham. This Round 34 clash promises to be intriguing given the disparity in league positions. The referee, Darren England, averages 3.7 yellow cards and 0.13 red cards per game, which could influence the physicality of this matchup.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, the results are fairly balanced with 1 win for Nottingham Forest, 2 draws, and 2 wins for Brentford. Their most recent encounter on December 21, 2024, saw Nottingham Forest triumph 2-0 away at Brentford, with Brentford’s Bryan Mbeumo earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.2 Sofascore rating despite the loss. Historically, head-to-head stats show Brentford have failed to keep a clean sheet in 11 consecutive games against Forest, while both teams have scored in 6 of their last 7 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Nottingham Forest's Form and Key Players
sections.text: Nottingham Forest have struggled defensively recently, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 home matches. However, their offensive output is led by Chris Wood, who ranks 4th in the league with 19 goals and has overperformed his expected goals (xG) by 7.6. Supporting him in attack is Anthony Elanga with 6 goals and 9 assists, while Morgan Gibbs-White adds creativity with 5 goals and 7 assists. Defensively, players like Murillo (7.18 average rating) and Nikola Milenković (4 goals as a defender) will be crucial. Unfortunately, Forest will miss defender Ola Aina due to injury, which could impact their backline stability.

sections.subtitle: Brentford's Form and Key Performers
sections.text: Brentford come into this game on a positive note, unbeaten in their last 3 matches across all competitions. Their attacking threat is spearheaded by Bryan Mbeumo, ranked 5th in the league with 18 goals and boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.51, complemented by 6 assists and ranking 5th in key passes with 65. Yoane Wissa supports with 17 goals, while Mikkel Damsgaard stands out with 10 assists, ranking 3rd in the league. Both Mbeumo and Wissa are in fine form over the last 5 matches, each scoring 3 goals. Defensively, Nathan Collins (7.21 rating) will be key to stopping Forest’s attack.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Team Stats
sections.text: Nottingham Forest excel in defensive metrics, ranking 4th for least goals conceded (41) and 2nd for clean sheets (13) in the league. They also lead the league in headers scored with 11 and clearances per game at 30.6. However, their possession game is weak, averaging just 39.9% (19th in the league). Brentford, on the other hand, have a knack for scoring late, with 28% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Forest’s goal distribution is more balanced, with significant scoring in the first 15 minutes (17%) and late in games (24.4% between 76-90 minutes).

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Streaks
sections.text: Nottingham Forest have a strong record of being the first to score in 4 of their last 5 head-to-head games against Brentford. Meanwhile, Brentford have seen both teams score in 4 of their last 5 away matches, suggesting an open game could be on the cards. Forest have also kept matches under 10.5 corners in 7 of their last 8 home games, indicating a potentially low-corner affair. Historically, Brentford’s inability to keep a clean sheet in 11 consecutive matches against Forest could play into the hosts’ hands.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: Given referee Darren England’s average of 3.7 yellow cards per game and Forest’s league rank of 9th for least yellow cards (72 total, 2.1 per game), we can expect a moderately disciplined game. Both teams have recent trends of matches with less than 4.5 cards, with Brentford showing this in 9 of their last 10 games and Forest in 5 of their last 7. As for set-pieces, Forest average 4.0 corners per game (16th in the league), and their trend of low corner counts at home suggests a conservative estimate for this match.
